user_query:
I’m preparing a small accessibility data pack for our next sprint review. Using the files under input/, please produce four outputs under out/:

1) Directory report (out/dir_report.txt): Recursively inspect input/ and write one line per file with: relative path, format, and a count. Use these counting rules: CSV = number of data rows (exclude header); JSONL = number of lines; HTML = number of <tr> rows in table#top-problems (exclude header); TXT = number of non-empty lines.

2) Combined issues table (out/issues_combined.csv): Extract issues from both sources and write a single CSV with header: source,session_id,issue_id,severity,description.
   - From input/sessions/notes.jsonl: flatten each issues[] entry into a row with source=notes; session_id from the record; issue_id from issues[i].id; severity lowercased; description trimmed.
   - From input/surveys/feedback.html: parse table#top-problems; for each body row create a row with source=survey; session_id=col1; issue_id=survey_top_problem; severity=lowercased col3 (if missing, default to medium); description=col2.
   No deduping across sources.

3) Missing sessions report (out/missing_sessions.csv): Using session IDs in input/sessions/roster.csv as the reference set, compare against session IDs present in notes.jsonl and in the survey table. Write a CSV with header: session_id,missing_in where missing_in is one of notes, survey, or both. Include only sessions that are missing from at least one source.

4) Plain-language email (out/email_rewrite.txt): Rewrite input/email_draft.txt to be accessible and concise. Requirements: at most 150 words total; the first line must start with "Subject:"; use clear, plain language; include a numbered list of exactly 3 action steps that cover (a) installing v1.3.0, (b) testing keyboard and screen reader navigation on key flows, and (c) sending feedback via the [survey link] or reply; preserve the exact deadline string "April 30, 2026" and the version string "v1.3.0".

Assume a standard local environment; no network calls. Place all outputs in the out/ folder and ensure all referenced inputs are actually read from input/ so results can be verified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
